rulings.Case Title: MARIA DELAFOSSE VS. PATRICIA KALMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion To Enforce Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Ccp Sec 998;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 29, 2011, Line 3, DEFENDANT PATRICIA KALMAN Notice Of And Motion To Enforce Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To CCP Sec 998. Granted. Plaintiff¿s offer was accepted by defendant in a signed writing prior to any purported revocation of the offer. Acceptance of the offer prior to revocation results in a binding agreement and, per CCP 998, judgment shall be entered. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: COIT VENTURE PARTNERS LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. 240 LOMBARD CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Requests For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 29, 2011, line 8, PLAINTIFF COIT VENTURE PARTNERS LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Requests For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions - Hearing required. The parties are directed to engage in further meet and confer in an effort to resolve or narrow the issues raised by the motion and report on their progress in doing so at the hearing. As guidance for the parties, the court¿s preliminary view is that defendant has not lost its work product privilege, but needs to product all non-privileged responsive documents if it hasn¿t done so already. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: DIGITAL SPEAR STREET LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, VS. 300 SPEAR REALTY PARTNERS, L.P., A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 29, 2011, Line 5, PLAINTIFF 360 SPEAR LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint. Granted. While defendant¿s arguments are normally better addressed through a demurrer or motion to strike, for the convenience of the parties and to avoid further pleading challenges raising the same arguments, the court has reviewed those arguments, and now rejects them. The new fraudulent conveyance claim is not barred, as a pleading matter, by the exculpatory clause, nor is the new claim premature. The new allegations regarding the failure to obtain the required insurance are not, as a pleading matter, defeated by the declaration of Mr. Shanagher. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DIMITRIOS STAVRAKIS VS. LILLIAN BAU LIN FAN AKA LILLIAN CHOU, AKA LILLIAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 29, 2011, Line 7, PLAINTIFF DIMITRIOS STAVRAKIS, PATRICIA MOLINA DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM PETER HANSEN DDS VS. OHIO NATIONAL LIFE ASSURANCE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 29, 2011, Line 8, DEFENDANT DAVE JONES DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff is granted leave, if he is able to do so in good faith, to allege that the Insurance Commissioner breached a specific mandatory duty, which the complaint does not now do. (Schwartz v. Poizner (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 572) A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant¿s exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also fails to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CYNTHIA FOSS VS. PETER BENNETT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 29, 2011, Line 12, DEFENDANT PETER BENNETT DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled with ten days leave to answer. The Marvin claim is adequately pled and the face of the complaint does not show that it is time-barred. The request for judicial notice submitted with the reply papers is denied as untimely because the request seeks to make a new argument not made in the moving papers and plaintiff lacked the opportunity to respond in writing to the request. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
